projectContext.summary: Default authorized profile for zero-knowledge and constraint-system audits. Add the target protocol's source, specs, books, papers, design docs, tests, and answer-free historical audit notes as corpus material.
criticalAssets: value and nullifier uniqueness when present
attackerCapabilities: A malicious prover can choose private witness values and try to satisfy the circuit with semantically inconsistent values.
A malicious prover can search for selector, rotation, lookup, copy, equality, range, or arithmetic gaps that let a false statement verify.

A malicious or faulty prover, coordinator, interpreter, witness source, or recovery-mode participant can try to bind a valid proof to the wrong task, block, metadata, verification key, or public input statement.
trustBoundaries: requested proof task identity to returned witness material
caller-provided gadget input to internal advice cells
reference equation to implemented gate polynomial
lookup table or range-check helper to dependent gadget logic
public input or instance column to private witness relation
chunk, batch, bundle, or recursive aggregation metadata to submitted proof bytes
coordinator task assignment and stored metadata to verifier submission
securityInvariants: Every witness value that affects a verified statement should be connected to the checks that rely on it.
Every proof statement should preserve the same requested task identity across witness checkout, serialization, proving, aggregation, coordinator storage, and verification.
Selectors and rotations should activate the intended constraints on every row where the security property is relied on.
Range, booleanity, lookup, copy, and equality relations should bind the same semantic value consumed by downstream checks.
Spec equations, exceptional-case assumptions, and protocol invariants should match the implemented gates and gadget composition.
focusAreas: proof task, witness source, metadata, and public-input binding
recursive aggregation proof and metadata binding
copy, equality, lookup, and instance-column relations
outOfScope: working exploit optimization beyond local proof of concept
scenarioGuidance: Do not write static bug rules that claim findings. Static extraction should produce only facts, obligations, and retrieval hints.
Keep deterministic checklist seeders disabled for blind proof runs so the model must enumerate the item before any audit trial can find it.
Give high priority to source-backed ingress, handoff, selector, lookup, range, and spec-obligation items when the audit budget is capped.

lensPacks.description: Audit how private witness values enter checked cells, committed values, accumulators, lookup inputs, and gadget outputs.
enumerationGuidance: Create items that connect each security-critical witness assignment to the nearest visible check, copy, equality, lookup, range, or public-input relation.
When a checked internal value depends on a caller-provided gadget input, enumerate that boundary separately from downstream arithmetic.
auditGuidance: Assignment code is the honest path, not proof of enforcement.
A positive finding needs a specific missing relation between the upstream semantic value and the checked internal value.

lensPacks.description: Audit zkVM/prover coordinator flows that transform requested tasks into witnesses, public inputs, proof metadata, recursive aggregation tasks, and verifier submissions.
enumerationGuidance: Create items that connect each requested task identity, block hash, batch hash, fork/version, or coordinator assignment to the returned witness and the derived public-input metadata.
Create separate items for chunk-to-batch, batch-to-bundle, and bundle-to-final-verifier aggregation boundaries when the source exposes them.
Prioritize submit-proof and verifier paths where proof bytes are wrapped with stored metadata or later used to update task, batch, or finalization state.
auditGuidance: A proof that verifies locally is not enough; check whether it proves the statement the coordinator requested.
Trace the same statement identity through witness fetch, serialized witness, metadata/public-input hash, proof wrapper, aggregation, verification key, and coordinator state update.
Explicitly separate normal coordinator paths from recovery, permissionless, or enforced-batch paths when attacker control differs.

lensPacks.description: Audit whether gates are active on the rows and rotations where their properties are relied on.
enumerationGuidance: Map selector enable calls, queried rotations, row offsets, and adjacent gadget handoffs.
Create follow-up items when a no-finding only proves a local equation but not the activation or neighboring boundary.
auditGuidance: Check selector coverage, row offsets, and rotations together with the property being enforced.
Do not assume a gate enforces a property outside the rows where its selector is active.

lensPacks.description: Audit values that must be bits, limbs, table members, canonical field elements, or bounded scalars.
enumerationGuidance: Find decompositions, running sums, limb splits, lookup inputs, table selectors, and canonicality checks.
Separate bit/limb recovery items from downstream arithmetic items when both are visible.
auditGuidance: Trace the same value from decomposition through range, lookup, boolean, and downstream equations.
Check overflow, truncation, reduction, endianness, and table-domain assumptions against the loaded reference material.

lensPacks.description: Audit reference equations and exceptional-case assumptions against implemented gate polynomials and gadget composition.
enumerationGuidance: Map each loaded equation, precondition, and exceptional-case rule to exact source lines for gate creation and assignment.
Prefer narrow items that pair one reference property with one visible implementation edge.
auditGuidance: Compare algebraic signs, rotations, selectors, substitutions, helper assumptions, and composition preconditions.
If a precondition is enforced by a caller or helper, identify that enforcement edge rather than relying on intent.
